description:
You are working as a freelance penetration-tester who has just been handed an Ubuntu-based target host that you can reach only from the same machine (the loop-back interface).  
    Your client wants a **very quick, one-shot discovery of TCP services** that are currently listening on the host.

    1. Run ONE suitable terminal command of your choice (for example an `nmap` service-detection scan) against **127.0.0.1**.
    2. Capture its raw, unmodified stdout output and save it to the file  
       /home/user/scan_results/localhost_scan.txt  
       (create the directory if it does not yet exist).
    3. The saved file **must follow this exact framing format** so that our automated grader can verify it:  
       • The first line must be exactly:  
         # Localhost Vulnerability Scan  
       • Beginning with the second line, write the untouched scan output that your command emitted.  
       • After the very last line of scan output, append the exact footer line:  
         # End of Scan  

       In other words, the file layout must be:

           # Localhost Vulnerability Scan
           <raw command output line 1>
           <raw command output line 2>
           ...
           <raw command output line N>
           # End of Scan

    4. Make no other edits to, or filtering of, the command output—just sandwich it between the required header and footer.
    5. The file must exist when you are finished, be a regular text file, and contain at least three total lines (header, one or more output lines, footer).

    When you are done you should be able to show the file on screen (e.g. with `cat /home/user/scan_results/localhost_scan.txt`) and see the header, the raw scan results, and the footer exactly as specified.
truth:
The autograder will verify all of the following:

    1. A regular file exists at the absolute path /home/user/scan_results/localhost_scan.txt
    2. The first line of this file is the exact ASCII string “# Localhost Vulnerability Scan” (without surrounding quotes, case-sensitive, no extra whitespace).
    3. The last line of this file is the exact ASCII string “# End of Scan”.
    4. The file contains at least one additional line between header and footer (i.e. total line-count ≥ 3).

    The grader will NOT check the specific content of the scan output itself, only that it is present between the two required delimiter lines.
name: task_000000_677665cc
